protocol.name: xfce_foreign_toplevel_management_private_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: xfce_foreign_toplevel_manager_private_v1
interface.description:
This protocol advertises extensions to the existing wlr-foreign-toplevel-management protocol, and provides a bridge to the ext-workspace protocol, so clients can learn what workspace each toplevel is on.
It is intended to be private to the Xfce desktop implementation, and should not be used by other applications. This protocol may introduce incompatible changes at any time, without warning, and without following normal, accepted guidelines about Wayland protocol versioning.

request.name: get_xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle
request.description:
This creates a new xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance for the given zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object.
On creation of the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance, all relevant property events will be emitted, followed by the done event, in order to communicate the initial state of the toplevel.
If a xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance is already bound to the provided wlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance, an already_bound protocol error is raised.
The lifecycle of this object is tied to the corresponding zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object. When the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1.closed event is emitted, the returned xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance will also become inert and should be destroyed.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Indicates to the compositor that the client no longer wishes to receive events on this interface.
The client still may receive more events that have been queued before the compositor processes this event.

description.summary: Protocol errors for the xfce_foreign_toplevel_manager_private_v1 interface

entry.name: already_bound
entry.summary: The wlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 already has a bound xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 instance

enum.name: state
enum.description: This enumeration includes toplevel states that are not a part of the base xdg-toplevel interface.

entry.name: shaded
entry.summary: The toplevel is 'rolled up' or 'shaded', usually meaning the window's title bar is visible, but the contents are not

entry.name: skip_pager
entry.summary: The toplevel should not be shown in the desktop pager view

entry.name: skip_tasklist
entry.summary: The toplevel should not be shown in a desktop task or window list

entry.name: sticky
entry.summary: The toplevel should be shown on all workspaces within its workspace group

entry.name: above
entry.summary: The toplevel should be stacked above other windows

entry.name: below
entry.summary: The toplevel should be stacked below other windows

entry.name: demands_attention
entry.summary: The toplevel has some urgent information waiting

request.name: set_sticky
request.description: A sticky toplevel appears on all workspaces in its workspace group.

request.name: set_above
request.description:
The window will be kept in a layer above other regularly-stacked windows.
This state is mutually-exclusive with the "below" state; setting the "above" state will unset "below" if it is set.

request.name: set_below
request.description:
The window will be kept in a layer below other regularly-stacked windows.
This state is mutually-exclusive with the "above" state; setting the "below" state will unset "above" if it is set.

request.name: move_to_output

description.summary: Moves the toplevel to another output

request.description:
This has no effect if the toplevel is already on the specified output.
The compositor may choose to ignore this request.

request.name: move_to_workspace

description.summary: Moves the toplevel to another workspace

request.description:
Requests that the compositor move the toplevel to the specified workspace.
Sticky windows cannot be moved to a particular workspace. The sticky state must first be unset.
The compositor may choose to ignore this request.

request.name: get_icon_pixels

description.summary: Requests pixel data for one of the toplevel's icons

request.description:
Requests an xfce_foreign_toplevel_icon_pixels_v1 handle for this toplevel.
The provided buffer must be a wl_shm buffer with format argb8888, and must be sized appropriately for the size and scale arguments. That is, the width and height must be equal to (scale * size), and the stride must be at least (scale * size * 4).
The size/scale pair must be a pair received in a previously received icon_size event, and that event must not have been obsoleted by a further icon event grouping.
The client must not modify or destroy the passed buffer until it has received the ready or failed events on the returned xfce_foreign_toplevel_icon_pixels_v1 instance.

arg.name: size
arg.summary: The width/height of the requested icon

arg.name: scale
arg.summary: The UI scale factor for the icon

arg.name: buffer
arg.summary: A wl_shm buffer at the appropriate size

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroys the object.
The client should call this when it no longer wishes to receive events for this toplevel, or after the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1.closed event has been received.

event.name: state

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel's state changes

event.description:
Reports the current state of the toplevel. This state should be combined with the state reported by wlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 in order to see the complete state of the toplevel.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent.

arg.name: state
arg.summary: List of active states from the state enum

event.name: icon_name

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel's icon name has changed

event.description:
If a themed icon name is set on this toplevel, this event will be emitted to inform clients of the toplevel's icon.
This event is mutually-exclusive with the icon_sizes and no_icon events, and receiving it overrides the other two states.
When a toplevel's icon changes, this event (or one of the other two) will be emitted within a "done" group. The client should treat any icon event grouping as fully replacing any previously-sent icon event grouping.
This event requires the xdg-toplevel-icon protocol, or a similar protocol that allows applications to associate icon names with their toplevels.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent.

event.name: icon_sizes

description.summary: Advertises the size and scale of a raster icon available for this toplevel

event.description:
If raw icon pixel data is set on this toplevel, this event will be emitted for each size/scale combination of icon data known to the compositor.
This event is mutually-exclusive with the icon_name and no_icon events, and receiving it overrides the other two states.
If the client wishes to fetch a particular size and scale of icon, it can use the get_icon_pixels request.
If the set of icon sizes/scales changes (that is, new scales/sizes are added, or some existing scales/sizes are removed), events describing the entire list of available sizes/scales will be re-emitted.
The entire set will also be re-emitted if the icon pixel data for any size/scale combination changes.
When a toplevel's icon changes, this event (or one of the other two) will be emitted within a "done" group. The client should treat any icon event grouping as fully replacing any previously-sent icon event grouping.
This event requires the xdg-toplevel-icon protocol, or a similar protocol that allows applications to associate icon pixel data with their toplevels.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent.

arg.name: size
arg.summary: Width and height of the icon, in pixels

arg.name: scale
arg.summary: The UI scale factor this pixel data corresponds to

event.name: no_icon

description.summary: Informs clients that the toplevel has no custom icon

event.description:
The client should instead use the toplevel's app_id as reported by wlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 in order to determine a suitable icon.
This event is mutually-exclusive with the icon_name and icon_sizes events, and receiving it overrides the other two states.
When a toplevel's icon changes, this event (or one of the other two) will be emitted within a "done" group. The client should treat any icon event grouping as fully replacing any previously-sent icon event grouping.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent.

event.name: workspace_enter

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel enters a workspace

event.description:
Emitted when this toplevel is placed on the specified workspace.
A toplevel can either be on only one workspace, or on all workspaces in a particular group.
If a workspace is made "sticky", that is, placed on all workspaces, this event will not be emitted for any workspaces. Instead, the state event will include the sticky state.
If the client has not yet bound the ext_workspace_manager_v1 global, and/or the ext_workspace_handle_v1 instances have not been created for the client, this event will be deferred until the workspace handle instances are created and available to the client.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent.

event.name: workspace_leave

description.summary: Emitted when the toplevel leaves a workspace

event.description:
Emitted when this toplevel is removed from the specified workspace.
A toplevel can either be on only one workspace, or on all workspaces in a particular group.
If a workspace is made "sticky", that is, placed on all workspaces, this event will be emitted for the previous single workspace it was on, followed by a new state event that includes the sticky state.
If the workspace referenced by the ext_workspace_handle_v1 is being destroyed, this event will be sent before the ext_workspace_handle_v1 itself is destroyed.
After all property changes have been made on this toplevel, the xfce_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1's done event will be sent.

event.name: done

description.summary: All pending property changes have been sent

event.description: Emitted when a group of property change events have all been sent.

interface.name: xfce_foreign_toplevel_icon_pixels_v1
interface.description: Provides the pixel data of a toplevel's icon.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Informs the compositor that the client no longer has a need for this instance.
If this request is called before the ready or failed events have been received, the compositor will consider the request for pixel data cancelled, and ensure it will not write to or read from the associated buffer after it has processed the destroy request. The ready and failed events will in this case not be emitted at all.

event.name: ready

description.summary: The icon's pixel data is ready for use

event.description:
The provided buffer has been populated with the icon's pixel data, and is ready for use.
The compositor will not update the contents of this buffer after this event is emitted. After emitting this event, the object is inert and should be destroyed.

event.name: failed

description.summary: The icon's pixel data could not be provided

event.description:
The icon's pixel data cannot be provided for the supplied reason.
The compositor will not update the contents of this buffer after this event is emitted. The buffer contents are undefined after receiving this event.
The client should destroy this object after receiving this event.

entry.name: no_pixel_data
entry.summary: The associated toplevel does not have any icon pixel data

entry.name: invalid_size
entry.summary: The specified size is not available

entry.name: invalid_scale
entry.summary: The specified scale is not available at the specified size

entry.name: invalid_buffer
entry.summary: The provided buffer did not meet the required constraints

entry.name: unknown
entry.summary: An unexpected error occurred populating the provided buffer's contents
